name: Canchal Negro Loop from La Covatilla
name_original: Canchal Negro por la Sierra de Béjar — circular desde La Covatilla: espacio Natural Sierra de Candelario
description: Hike the moderate 5.4-mile Canchal Negro circular route from La Covatilla, gaining 1684 feet for panoramic views and a lunar landscape.
region_intro: Hiking around Navacarros offers diverse landscapes within the province of Ávila, characterized by mountainous terrain and striking rock formations. The region features dense pine forests and areas with chestnut trees, particularly along trails like the Camino de las Cascadas. These natural features provide varied environments for outdoor activities, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Navacarros The most popular hiking route is Canchal Negro Loop from La Covatilla, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Route of the Fountains of Candelario - circular through the Natural Space of the Sierra de Candelario, a difficult 3.6 miles (5.7 km) path. This trail winds through the natural space of the Sierra de Candelario, featuring various water sources. Local hikers also love the Fuente de la Hoja – Ermita del Humilladero loop from Béjar, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through varied countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Navacarros is defined by mountainous landscapes, rock formations, and trails through pine and chestnut forests, including paths with waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to difficult ascents. The routes in Navacarros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Navacarros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navacarros?
A: There are over 80 hiking trails around Navacarros, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to more challenging ascents through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Navacarros?
A: Hiking near Navacarros offers diverse landscapes, including mountainous terrain, striking rock formations, and dense pine forests. You'll also encounter areas with beautiful chestnut trees, especially along paths like the Camino de las Cascadas, which are particularly vibrant in certain seasons. The region is part of the broader Sierra de Béjar, providing a rich natural environment.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls near Navacarros?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="#" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de las Cascadas&lt;/a&gt; (Waterfall Path) is a notable trail just a few minutes from Navacarros. It winds through tall pine trees and native vegetation, offering enchanting views of waterfalls and a diverse color palette, especially with its chestnut trees.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hikes around Navacarros?
A: The trails around Navacarros cater to various abilities. You'll find approximately 16 easy routes for leisurely strolls, 45 moderate trails for a good workout, and 27 difficult paths for experienced hikers seeking a challenge. The area's mountainous nature means some routes involve significant ascents.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes around Navacarros are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1141520663/canchal-negro-loop-from-la-covatilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal Negro Loop from La Covatilla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10371657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Hoja – Ermita del Humilladero loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; also offers a varied countryside experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover on a hike?
A: You can discover several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5432000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Reservoir Views&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic vistas. For historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1062938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Humilladero&lt;/a&gt; is a religious building often included in routes. More challenging hikes might lead you to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón (Calvitero Summit)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options near Navacarros?
A: Yes, Navacarros offers several family-friendly hiking options, particularly among the easier routes. These trails often feature gentle gradients and scenic points of interest suitable for all ages. The diverse natural settings, including pine and chestnut forests, provide an engaging environment for families to explore together.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Navacarros?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience around Navacarros, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from dense forests to mountainous vistas, and the tranquility of the natural surroundings. The presence of features like the Camino de las Cascadas also receives positive feedback.

Q: Is Navacarros a good destination for dog-friendly walks?
A: Navacarros is generally suitable for dog-friendly walks, with many trails winding through natural landscapes like forests and open areas. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navacarros?
A: Navacarros offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors, especially when the chestnut trees are in full display. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter offers a different, often tranquil, experience in the mountainous landscape, though some higher trails might require appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993362661/the-route-of-the-fountains-of-candelario-circular-through-the-natural-space-of-the-sierra-de-candelario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Fountains of Candelario&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult path winding through the Sierra de Candelario. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1275875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossroads and beautiful views – Views on the embalse de Navamuño loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a significant distance and elevation.

Q: What are the options for parking when hiking near Navacarros?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Navacarros and nearby towns typically offer public parking facilities. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking spots, especially near popular trailheads or village centers.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails near Navacarros using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Navacarros and its surrounding hiking areas may be limited. While local bus services might connect to larger towns, direct access to all trailheads via public transport is not always available. Planning your journey in advance and considering local taxi services or carpooling is recommended for reaching more remote starting points.
tips: Canchal Negro is a point in Salamanca and has an elevation of 2,369 meters. It is located to the east of Sierra de Candelario.

Formerly before the ski resort and the new road, when the mountaineers went up the mountains, they stayed to sleep here or took refuge from bad weather.

Früher wenn is keine Skistation oder schone Asphaltstrasse gabt, sind die Wanderer Hier beim schlechten Wetter oder zum Übernachten hier geblieben.

faqs.question: Where does the Canchal Negro circular trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently starts from the La Covatilla ski station. You'll find parking facilities available there, making it an accessible starting point for exploring the high mountains of southern Salamanca.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Canchal Negro por la Sierra de Béjar circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll traverse a granitic massif with rounded summits, encounter areas dense with *piornal* (broom) and *matorral* (shrubland), and navigate rocky sections. As you ascend towards the Canchal Negro Summit, the landscape transforms into a unique 'lunar landscape' of grey, stony expanses and granite boulders.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time depends on your preference. In summer, the ascent from La Covatilla can be easier, sometimes along the edges of ski slopes. In winter, the rounded relief of Canchal Negro makes it popular for ski mountaineering and snowshoeing. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Canchal Negro circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly as it's located within the Espacio Natural Sierra de Candelario. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially in areas with livestock or dense vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to hike in Espacio Natural Sierra de Candelario?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for general hiking activities within the Espacio Natural Sierra de Candelario. However, always check local regulations for any specific requirements, especially for large groups or organized events.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning panoramic views throughout the hike. From various natural viewpoints, you can observe the dramatic Garganta del Oso (Bear's Gorge). The Canchal Negro Summit offers an excellent lookout over the Castilian plateau to the north and the majestic Sierra de Gredos to the east.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes segments that are part of the [CIMA SA08] La Covatilla trail, covering approximately 8% of its length. This means you might encounter other hikers or cyclists using parts of this established route.

faqs.question: Are there any historical or notable landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll pass by the ruins of the Old Covatilla Refuge near the starting point. The trail itself is a significant geographical landmark, and the unique 'lunar landscape' around Canchal Negro is a striking natural feature.

faqs.question: How challenging is the ascent through the pine forest?
faqs.answer: The ascent through the pine forest, specifically by a ridge separating two stream valleys, is considered one of the tougher parts of this trek. It contributes to the trail's overall 'moderate' to 'medium-high' difficulty rating.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding navigation on this trail?
faqs.answer: While it's a circular route, some sections can be challenging to navigate. Paths may be obscured by dense vegetation, especially in areas with *piornal* and *matorral*. Following cairns (*hitos*) is often necessary to stay on track, particularly in less defined areas.

faqs.question: Are there any natural water features or places to cool off in the area?
faqs.answer: Further into the natural space, around the Arroyo del Oso (Bear Stream), you can find evocative landscapes with waterfalls, rapids, and natural pools like El Cubo, La Bañera, and La Chorrera Blanca. These are popular for bathing in summer, despite the cold water.
